ingredients.name: La fermata
ingredients.entityContext: This restaurant in the Yuwaku area appears in episode 2 as a location visited by characters in the Kanazawa outskirts. It is documented as a pilgrimage spot in anime location guides matching the show's background art.

ingredients.name: Siii
ingredients.entityContext: This Kanazawa-area spot appears in episode 2 and is listed in pilgrimage guides as a confirmed background location. Its exterior matches the anime's depiction of local establishments near Yuwaku Onsen.

ingredients.name: Yuwaku Inari Shrine
ingredients.address: Yuwakuinari Shrine, I-145 Yuwakumachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-1123, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small shrine in Yuwaku Onsen appears in episode 4 and is one of the most photographed pilgrimage spots for Hanasaku Iroha fans, representing the quiet spiritual atmosphere of the hot spring village that shapes Ohana's new life. Pilgrims visit to recreate the anime's scene compositions at the torii gate.

ingredients.name: Nishigishi Post Office

ingredients.name: Saigawa-Ohashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Saigawa-Ōhashi Bridge, 1 Chome-1 Nomachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 921-8031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This iconic Kanazawa bridge over the Sai River appears in episode 7 and is a well-documented pilgrimage location in the city. The bridge's iron railings and river view match the show's backgrounds when characters travel between Kanazawa and Yuwaku.

ingredients.name: Kanazawa Yuwaku Community Center

ingredients.address: Kanazawashi Yuwaku Community Center, I-５９ Shibaharamachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-1129, Japan

ingredients.name: Saikawa Bridge Police Box
ingredients.localName: 金沢中警察署 犀川大橋交番
ingredients.entityContext: This police box near Saigawa-Ohashi Bridge in Kanazawa appears in episode 8 and is documented in pilgrimage location comparisons as a background detail in city scenes. It sits within the broader Kanazawa pilgrimage cluster fans walk.

ingredients.name: Shiinoki Cultural Complex
ingredients.localName: 石川県政記念しいのき迎賓館
ingredients.address: Shiinoki Cultural Complex, しいのき迎賓館内 1F 2 Chome-1-1 Hirosaka, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0962, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Kanazawa landmark near Kenroku-en appears in episode 8 and is recognizable in the anime's background art for city scenes. Its distinctive zelkova trees and colonial-era architecture feature in pilgrimage photo comparisons by fans.

ingredients.name: Omicho Market
ingredients.address: Omicho Market, 50 Kamiomicho, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0905, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kanazawa's historic covered market appears in episode 21 and captures the lively everyday city life that contrasts with the quiet Yuwaku onsen village. Ohana and colleagues are seen here in a market scene that showcases the town's seafood culture.

ingredients.name: Biblio.Classic
ingredients.localName: ビブリオ. クラシック
ingredients.address: Biblio.Classic, サンライズビル ３Ｆ 3 Chome-7-2 Zoshigaya, Toshima City, Tokyo 171-0032, Japan

ingredients.name: Docomo Tower
ingredients.localName: NTTドコモ代々木ビル(ドコモタワー)
ingredients.address: Docomo Tower, 5 Chome-24-10 Sendagaya, Shibuya, Tokyo 151-0051, Japan

ingredients.name: Tokyo Tower
ingredients.address: Tokyo Tower, 4 Chome-2-8 Shibakoen, Minato City, Tokyo 105-0011
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Tower appears in episode 17 as a background landmark during scenes set in Tokyo, where Ohana's free-spirited mother Satsuki lives. The tower visually marks the contrast between the bustling city life Ohana left behind and the quiet inn world she has grown to love.

ingredients.name: Kanazawa Yuwaku Yumeji-kan
ingredients.address: Kanazawa Yuwaku Yumeji-kan, I-144 Yuwakumachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-1123, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Located steps from Yuwaku Onsen, this museum dedicated to artist Yumeji Takehisa appears in episode 26 and is part of the core Yuwaku pilgrimage cluster. Its Meiji-era aesthetic matches the nostalgic mood Ohana reflects on as the Bonbori Festival approaches.

ingredients.name: Grass Matinee Kanazawa
ingredients.address: Kanazawa, Ishikawa, Japan

ingredients.name: Nishigishi Station
ingredients.address: I Nakajimamachisode, Nanao, Ishikawa 929-2213, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small rural station on the Noto Railway is the real-world model for Yuina-machi Station, where Ohana Matsumae arrives alone in episode 1 to begin her new life at Kissuiso. The weathered wooden platform captures the same quiet, isolated feeling Ohana experiences as she takes her first uncertain steps into her new world.

ingredients.name: Roppongi Hills
ingredients.address: 6 Chome-10-1 Roppongi, Minato City, Tokyo 106-6108, Japan

ingredients.name: Sunshine City
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-1-2 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-8630, Japan

ingredients.name: Tokyo Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome Marunouchi, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 100-0005, Japan

ingredients.name: Kanazawa Station
ingredients.address: 1番-1号 Kinoshinbomachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0858, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kanazawa Station's famous Tsuzumi-mon drum gate appears in episode 8 as Ohana and other characters pass through the city. It serves as the main transit hub for anyone making the pilgrimage journey from Tokyo to Yuwaku Onsen.

ingredients.name: Yuwaku Post Office
ingredients.entityContext: The Yuwaku Post Office appears in the opening sequence and is one of the landmark buildings fans spot while walking the village streets that inspired Kissuiso's surroundings. Its small-town feel anchors the rural onsen setting Ohana calls home.

ingredients.name: Omotesando Illumination
ingredients.address: Omotesando Hills, 4 Chome-12-10 Jingumae, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Fukujin Bridge
ingredients.address: Fukujin Bridge, Shintoku, Kamikawa District, Hokkaido, Japan

ingredients.name: Gyokusen Kobo
ingredients.entityContext: This craft studio in Yuwaku Onsen appears in the ending credits, representing the quiet artisan character of the hot spring village. Fans walk past it as part of the Yuwaku Onsen pilgrimage loop.

ingredients.name: Gyokusendo (Yuwaku Onsen)
ingredients.localName: 湯涌温泉総湯 白鷺の湯
ingredients.address: Yuwaku Onsen Public Bath, イ１３９-2 Yuwakumachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-1123, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This public bathhouse in Yuwaku Onsen, known as Shirasagi-no-Yu, is featured in the ending credits sequence and embodies the everyday onsen life at the heart of the series. Its traditional exterior is a beloved photo stop for fans completing the Hanasaku Iroha pilgrimage circuit.

ingredients.name: Drug SEIJO Hitotsubashi-Gakuen Station Front Shop
ingredients.address: Hitotsubashi-gakuen Station, 2 Chome-1-1 Gakuen Nishimachi, Kodaira, Tokyo 187-0045, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The area around Hitotsubashi-gakuen Station in Tokyo appears in episode 1 as part of the neighborhood where Ohana lives before she is sent to Yuwaku. It establishes the urban starting point of her journey.

ingredients.name: Motoyoshi Shokuryohinten
ingredients.address: Tempura Motoyoshi, Granbell Ebisu IV, 3階 2 Chome-8-11 Ebisunishi, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Yuwaku-area shop appears in episode 1 in the context of establishing the inn's local surroundings. It is documented in pilgrimage records as a background location match near Kokarian and the Yuwaku cluster.

ingredients.name: Kokarian
ingredients.address: Kokarian, I-６８ Yuwakumachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-1123, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This atmospheric Yuwaku Onsen ryokan directly inspired the look and spirit of Kissuiso, the inn where Ohana works under her strict grandmother Sui in episode 1. Walking its hallways gives fans the closest real-life feel for the show's central setting.
